info.title: OPTCG Card Search API
info.description: API specification for the OPTCG Card Search single-page application after the visual redesign (ADR-1). This is a client-side SPA backed by static JSON data. There are no new HTTP endpoints introduced by the redesign. This spec documents: (1) the SPA route contract used by react-router-dom, (2) the existing static data endpoint, (3) the x-agent-api extension for machine-to-machine use. Route changes: '/' is now LandingPage (was previously also CardSearchPage); '/search' replaces '/cards' as the search results route.

tags.name: routes
tags.description: SPA client-side routes rendered by react-router-dom

tags.name: data
tags.description: Static JSON data endpoints served by the Node.js backend

get.description: Renders the LandingPage component. Shows a centered logo, tagline, and search bar. No card data is loaded on this route. On search submit, the browser navigates to /search?q={query}. This route was previously shared with the card search results view; after the redesign it is a dedicated landing page.

200.description: HTML shell returned by the SPA server; React renders LandingPage.

schema.description: Full HTML document with React root. The actual UI is client-rendered.

get.description: Renders the SearchPage component (renamed from CardSearchPage). Reads query parameters on mount via the useCardUrlSync hook and loads card data if not yet loaded. Previously served at /cards; that path now redirects here for backwards compatibility.
get.x-theme: light or dark (inherited from useTheme hook)

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search query. Searches card name, effect text, and card number. Case-insensitive. Example: 'Luffy' or 'OP01-001'.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: Filter by card color. Accepted values match the OPTCG card color constants. Case-sensitive.

parameters.name: set
parameters.description: Filter by card set code (e.g. 'OP01', 'OP07'). Values are set codes from the card data.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: Display mode for search results. 'grid' shows the 5-column card thumbnail grid; 'list' shows the compact row list.

200.description: HTML shell returned by the SPA server; React renders SearchPage with the given query parameters.

schema.description: Full HTML document. Query parameters are read by useCardUrlSync on mount.

get.description: Backwards-compatible redirect. Any request to /cards (with or without query parameters) is redirected by react-router-dom's <Navigate to='/search' replace> to /search. Query parameters are NOT automatically forwarded by the React redirect; this path is maintained only for bookmarks that lack query parameters. Deep-linked URLs with query params (e.g. /cards?q=Luffy) should migrate to /search?q=Luffy.
get.x-deprecation-note: Use /search instead. This route will be removed in a future version.

302.description: Client-side redirect to /search via react-router-dom Navigate component.

get.description: Returns the complete OPTCG card catalog as a JSON array. This endpoint is called once on SearchPage mount via the cardDataProvider. The data is loaded from a static JSON file on the server; there is no database. This endpoint is UNCHANGED by the redesign.
get.x-notes: This endpoint is unchanged by the visual redesign. It is documented here for completeness and for the developer agent's reference.

500.description: Server error reading card data file.

Card.description: A single OPTCG card. All fields reflect the existing card data schema; no new fields are added by the redesign.

id.description: Card number in the format SET-NNN (e.g. 'OP01-001').

name.description: Card name as printed on the card.

cost.description: Play cost. Null for Leader cards.

power.description: Card power value. Null for Event/Stage cards.

counter.description: Counter value. Null if the card has no counter.

life.description: Life points. Only present for Leader cards.

traits.description: List of trait strings (e.g. ['Straw Hat Crew', 'Supernovas']).

effect.description: Full card effect text as printed on the card.
effect.example: Activate: Main Once Per Turn Give up to 1 of your Characters...

rarity.description: Card rarity (C, UC, R, SR, L, SEC).

imageUrl.description: URL of the card art image. May be a relative or absolute URL.

alternateArts.description: List of alternate art image URLs. Empty array if no alternates exist.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error envelope. All errors from the API use this shape.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.
message.example: Could not load the card catalog. The data file may be missing or corrupted.

suggestion.description: Actionable suggestion for the user or caller.
suggestion.example: Try refreshing the page. If the problem persists, check that the server is running correctly.

field.description: The specific field that caused a VALIDATION_ERROR. Omitted for non-validation errors.

ThemePreference.description: User theme preference, stored in localStorage under the key 'optcg-theme'. If absent, the system preference (prefers-color-scheme) is used.

SearchState.description: The search filter state encoded in the /search URL query string and managed by useCardUrlSync. This is not an HTTP request body — it describes the URL parameter contract.

error.message: Could not load the card catalog.
error.suggestion: Try refreshing the page. If the problem persists, check your internet connection.

error.message: The page you are looking for does not exist.
error.suggestion: Return to the home page and try searching from there.

x-route-table.description: Complete react-router-dom route table after the redesign. Matches blueprint section 1.

routes.description: Dedicated landing page with centered search bar. No card data loaded.
Full search and filter results page. Reads URL params on mount via useCardUrlSync.
Preserves old bookmarks. Query params are NOT forwarded.
Any unrecognised path redirects to the landing page.

x-theme-system.description: CSS custom property theme system. Not an HTTP API — documented here for developer agent reference.
x-theme-system.html_class: html.light or html.dark applied synchronously by inline <script> in index.html <head>
